respStmt.resp: University Archivist and Director of Special Collections

div.p: FORT BRAGG. N. C. OCC #56 St. Sill Okla. Jan 6, 1942

Dear Mother + Dad It's 10:00 o'clock and I've just finished studging. I'll try to dash off a letter before lights out at 10:30. For some strange reason we had no classes Monday morning -- and so I was able to get to the part office for the watch. It's just what I hoped it would be. All the fellows are properly envious. It's keeping perfect time, and I find myself admiring it constantly. Thank you so much.

Mine certainly was a Xmas worth waiting for. I have so much food I don't know where to store it all and I can't eat it all -- neither can the fellows. In addition to your fine box of food, for which I also thank you, I have the Groover's box, a 5 lb box of candy from Aunt Nelle, a box of hard candies from Mrs T., + lb or 2 lb box from the peope in the Registrar's Office on the hill. The fellows

say they have never seen so much food in their lives If you see any of those who sent me things, tell them how much I appreciated it. I’m writing of course, too. klaks for the iron -- it was just the type I wanted. Unfortunately, I discovered, I can't use it here because of strain on the electric system -- but I can use it later at some other camp so it's not wasted.

Handkies, of course, always come in handy. I've received another huge lunch of Xmas greetings forwarded from Bragg. Time doesn't permit my listing all of the senders. When I send the box home (after I empty your tins), you can look through then.

Please send Grandma's money by money order. I don't get paid until Jan 30th (2 months pay then) and I've had a lot of expense. ($ 10.95 foot locker -- suitcase -- a few books + supplies -- etc. Also send my electric razor if you haven't sold it. I guess I got through Motors O.K? I got all 5's anyway. How I don't know. We had our 1st Material test today. I got an 5. Also saw

the latest F.A. gun today It's a honey. It's still a military secret -- but it looks plenty powerful. Received papers. Thanks. What is D. Griffith's address, or her husbands? I'd like to look them up if I have a chance.

Young (my roommate) is from about 100 miles upstate from Sill. He is 28, married, and expecting to pass out cigars an day now. Zavodnik is 24. We plan to take picture this Sunday. When we do + get them developed -- I'll send them on to you. Lawton is 4 miles from Sill. Nice homes. Reminds me of Ambry. Wide streets and all.

Have not received Readers Digest. Will write + thank Mr. T. when I do. Did receive Showalter's card along with many others. My schedule is really getting tough. Up at 6:00. Clean up. Compulsary breakfast. Drill 8-9. Classes 9-1 Lunch 1:15, classes 2-6, Supper 6:30. Compulsary study 7:15 to 10:00. Lights out 10:30. It takes all of my time just to do the things I have to do. No time to relax or read things I want to.
I'll close now til Sunday. thanks again for everything. have to the best parents in the world - John
